## Summary

The `WineCellar` spec is a card definition that represents a wine cellar. It provides a comprehensive view of a wine cellar, including details about the cellar itself, the bottles it contains, and various statistics and visualizations.

## Import

```js
import { WineCellar } from '../wine-cellar';
```

## Usage as a Field

The `WineCellar` spec can be used as a field within a consuming card or field. To use it, simply include the `@fields.wineCellar` helper in your template:

```hbs
<@fields.wineCellar />
```

This will render the wine cellar in the consuming component's template.

## Template Usage

To use the `WineCellar` spec in a template, you can invoke the various format templates it provides:

```hbs
{{! Isolated format }}
<WineCellar @format="isolated" />

{{! Embedded format }}
<WineCellar @format="embedded" />

{{! Fitted format }}
<WineCellar @format="fitted" />

{{! Atom format }}
<WineCellar @format="atom" />
```

The `WineCellar` spec provides the following formats:

- `isolated`: A detailed view of the wine cellar, including a timeline of the bottles, statistics, and a list of the bottles.
- `embedded`: A compact view of the wine cellar, displaying key information such as the total number of bottles, total value, and average score.
- `fitted`: A minimal view of the wine cellar, showing the name, location, and total number of bottles.
- `atom`: An inline representation of the wine cellar, showing the name and total number of bottles.

The appropriate format can be chosen based on the layout and context of the consuming component.
